steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The blank describes what some drivers believe about the current toll — specifically the belief that makes them impossible to persuade, no matter how much justification is offered for raising it. The word must capture a conviction so strong it blocks any argument.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'contentious': means 'likely to cause disagreement.' A toll can be contentious without drivers believing it is already too high — this doesn't explain why they'd resist any increase. ✗
  • Choice B — 'equivocal': means 'ambiguous or unclear.' Drivers who think the toll is ambiguous aren't necessarily resistant to a raise. ✗
  • Choice C — 'exorbitant': means 'unreasonably or excessively high.' If drivers already believe the toll is exorbitant, no justification for raising it further could persuade them — that is exactly the unmovable position the passage describes. ✓
  • Choice D — 'warranted': means 'justified.' Drivers who think the current toll is warranted might actually accept a modest increase; this is the opposite of the resistant stance the blank requires. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
